item.title: Yoga for scoliosis can be life-changing | Letters
item.summary: I’ve worked to cope with the chronic pain through ashtanga yoga, says Louise Zamparutti . Plus letters from Kirie Pedersen and Alice Devon Thank you for publishing Natasha Livingstone’s article ( A moment that changed me: I started yoga – and saw my scoliosis in a surprising new light, 15 July ). I have been ashamed of my scoliosis my entire life. It wasn’t diagnosed until my early 20s, and by that time I had begun a career as a dancer and choreographer. My dance teachers were always criticising my off-kilter posture and I constantly felt that I didn’t deserve to pursue a career in dance due to my disfigured spine. My curvature is both thoracic and lumbar, and runs both sideways and forward-backward. I, too, have an uneven waistline, a hip curve on one side and straight rib-to-hip line on the other. Pants fit unevenly on me, and for years I would never wear an open-backed top. Reading this piece made me recognise how hard I’ve worked to cope with the chronic pain, through ashtanga yoga, which I taught for over 10 years, and now through lap swimming. But I haven’t come to terms with the shame. This piece gave me the sense that I am not alone – that there are others of us who have sc
